title: Violet creams
subtitle: A floral fondant centre coated in dark chocolate
excerpt: Violet creams are a classic British confectionery: a soft fondant centre flavoured with violet extract, shaped into ovals, and dipped in dark chocolate. The fondant is made from icing sugar and liquid glucose, which prevents crystallisation and gives the centre its characteristic smooth melt.
sourceNotes: Violet creams are a documented British confectionery form from the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries, produced commercially from around 1900. The fondant-with-violet-flavouring dipped in chocolate is recorded in public-domain British confectionery manuals from the Edwardian era.

recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe; the fondant and dipping process scale straightforwardly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The finished chocolates keep in a cool, dry place for up to 2 weeks, stored in a single layer or with paper between layers.

glossaryTerms.definition: Liquid glucose (glucose syrup) is added to icing-sugar fondant to prevent the sugar from crystallising as the fondant sets. Without it, the fondant centre dries to a grainy, coarse texture rather than a smooth, silky one. Glucose interferes with sucrose crystal formation, keeping the mass pliable and uniform. A small amount, around 10 to 15 per cent of the total sugar weight, is sufficient.

content.text: Violet creams are one of the few confectioneries that require no cooking at all. The fondant is made by working icing sugar and

together with a small amount of water and violet extract until a smooth, pliable mass forms. The centres are shaped, left to firm slightly, then dipped in melted dark chocolate. The hardest part of the recipe is finding culinary violet extract rather than decorative violet flavouring, which tends to taste synthetic.

Put the sifted icing sugar in a bowl. Add the liquid glucose, water, and violet extract. Mix with a spoon, then knead with your hands until the mixture comes together into a smooth, pliable dough. If it is too dry, add water half a teaspoon at a time. Taste; add more violet extract if needed. Roll into a log and pinch off pieces about the size of a large marble. Shape each into an oval. Place on baking paper and leave to firm at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Melt the dark chocolate in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Remove from the heat and leave to cool to about 32 degrees C, stirring occasionally. Drop each fondant centre into the chocolate, turn to coat, and lift out with a fork. Let the excess drip off, then place on baking paper. Leave to set at room temperature; do not refrigerate. Once set, the coating will be firm and slightly shiny.
